ground_truth.title: Best Online Certificate Programs
ground_truth.author: Alison Plaut
ground_truth.main_content: Best Online Certificate Programs. Online certificate programs can help you stand out in a competitive job market and build skills without the time and cost of a degree. Find options here. Whether you're eager to boost your skills for a new career or level up in your current one, certificate programs are a quick and affordable way to do so. They offer training in many fields, including healthcare and technology. An online format means you can earn these credentials without sacrificing your job or other personal obligations. Plus, certificates cost less than a four-year degree, and the credits may transfer to an undergraduate or graduate program. Explore some of the best online certificate programs to see what's right for you. Why Earn a Certificate Online? Learn specialized skills in just 3-12 months — much faster than a bachelor's program. Juggle school, work, and other commitments more easily with the flexibility of online learning. Save money on transportation, relocation, and other expenses you would incur with a traditional program. Upskill in your field, or pivot to a new career. Top Accredited Schools Offering Online Certificate Programs: Southern New Hampshire University offers four undergraduate and 19 graduate certificate programs online. These programs span fields such as marketing, human resource management, and computer programming. SNHU's certificates are career-focused and designed to enhance existing degrees. Ohio State University offers nine undergraduate and 20 graduate certificates online, catering to various academic interests and professional goals. The University of Virginia offers four undergraduate and 10 graduate certificates via distance education for working adults. The University of Florida stands out for its diverse online certificate options, with 98 graduate and 12 undergraduate programs. North Carolina State University offers 18 undergraduate and 47 graduate certificate programs fully online in fields such as engineering, business, and the sciences. Boston University offers 36 online graduate certificates in fields such as art education, crime analysis, and financial management. Texas A&M University has more than 60 online certificate programs geared toward working professionals and current students. The University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill offers 10 online undergraduate and graduate certificate programs in business, journalism, law, public health, and more. The University of Georgia has 22 online undergraduate and graduate certificate programs in fields including education, leadership, law, and science.
